title: Requirement-Driven Model Change + Peripheral Updates
description: Agent reads a changed requirement (REQ_SCS_007), modifies the model to implement it, and updates all associated peripheral files (interface JSON, MIL test JSON) in a single workflow.

steps.title: Implement the requirement change
steps.prompt: A new requirement REQ_SCS_007 has been added to requirements: "The corrective yaw moment shall be derated as a function of vehicle speed using a calibratable 1-D lookup table. At low speeds (below 20 km/h), intervention authority shall be reduced to 30% to prevent uncomfortable jerking during parking maneuvers. At high speeds (above 200 km/h), authority shall be limited to 60% to avoid destabilizing overcorrection. Full authority (100%) applies in the range 60 to 160 km/h. A derating factor output shall indicate the current authority level." Implement this change in the model and update all peripheral files.
steps.notes: Key moment: Multi-artifact atomic workflow — model + interface + tests all updated in one pass. Known issue: Lookup_n-D block NumberOfTableDimensions may not apply correctly via model_edit add_block. If LUT shows ? icon or has 2 input ports, agent should use evaluate_matlab_code with set_param.
look_for: Agent reads the model (model_overview → model_read) to find where to insert
Agent uses model_edit to add 1-D Lookup Table, Product block, output port inside StabilityController
Agent configures LUT breakpoints [0, 5.56, 16.67, 44.44, 55.56, 70] m/s and table values [0.3, 0.3, 1.0, 1.0, 0.6, 0.6]
Agent uses edit_file to update interface.json and mil_test.json
Agent uses model_read to verify the new structure
steps.recovery: If agent adds blocks at root level instead of inside StabilityController, prompt: 'The derating should be implemented inside the StabilityController subsystem, between the EnableSwitch and the ControlMomentOut output port'

presentation.background: A new requirement REQ_SCS_007 mandates speed-dependent derating of corrective yaw moment. The agent must implement this in the model and update all peripheral artifacts atomically.
good_looks_like: 1-D Lookup Table block: VehicleSpeedCtrl input → derating factor (0.3 / 1.0 / 0.6)
Product block: EnableSwitch output × derating factor → ControlMomentOut
New output port: DeratingFactorOut routed to root-level output
interface.json updated with SpeedDeratingBP_mps, SpeedDeratingTable inputs and DeratingFactor output
mil_test.json updated with derating test cases at low/mid/high speeds linked to REQ-SCS-007
